methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $15.5B

data.narrative:
Climeworks is a Series F-stage direct air capture company founded by Christoph Gebald and Jan Wurzbacher. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes one public comparable (Linde, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the private comparable universe lacks sufficient depth at identical or adjacent stages to support a reliable peer-median approach; only one same-stage peer was identified across the narrower filter criteria.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement to the company's $1.0B total capital raised proxy. This approach yields an implied valuation of $1.3B. The stage-progression method is appropriate given the company's capital intensity and developmental phase, though its application depends on the assumption that $1.0B raised represents a reasonable proxy for prior valuation.

The triangulated range is $1.1B–$1.5B (median $1.3B), supported by a single methodological input. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust private peer set for cross-validation, the representativeness of the $1.0B raised proxy as a valuation anchor, and the appropriate step-up multiple for this specific technology and market stage. Additional comparables at Series E or Series G would materially improve conviction in this range.
